task_completion.description: Open the 'world_survey.gpkg' project in the GIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIS application (e.g., QField) is open and the 'world_survey' project or layers are visible in the interface.

task_completion.description: Locate and select the Cairo capital city feature to view its coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map is centered on Cairo, or the feature attributes/identify results for Cairo are displayed on the screen.

task_completion.description: Start creating a new point in the 'field_observations' layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'field_observations' layer is selected for editing and the 'add feature' or 'digitize' mode is active.

task_completion.description: Place the new point exactly at Cairo's location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The crosshair or new point marker is positioned over the existing Cairo feature on the map before confirming the geometry.
The attribute form for the new point shows 'QA_Log_Cairo' entered into the name or title field.

task_completion.description: Record the calculated deviation in the description field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The attribute form shows exactly 'dLat: 0.0026, dLon: -0.0020' entered into the description field.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the GIS application to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with QField/GIS app menus, map canvas, and attribute forms, rather than terminal windows executing sqlite3 commands.

integrity.description: The deviation values were entered into the actual feature attribute form.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'dLat: 0.0026, dLon: -0.0020' is visible inside the GIS application's native input fields, not in a separate text editor or note-taking app.

integrity.description: The point was placed using the map interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is visual evidence of the map view with a crosshair or digitizing tool being used to set the point's location.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The true map coordinates for Cairo in the database are Latitude: 30.0626, Longitude: 31.2480. The benchmark coordinates are Lat: 30.0600, Lon: 31.2500. The correct calculated deviation (Map_Value - Benchmark_Value) is exactly 'dLat: 0.0026, dLon: -0.0020'. The target layer is 'field_observations' and the point name must be 'QA_Log_Cairo'.
